{{Quote|The Empire has left behind many troops to keep control of Cloud City. We must enter the city and remove the Imperial presence.|[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]]|<ref name="SW:BF" />}}
The '''Battle of Bespin''' took place in the time around the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Prelude==
The [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], with its [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] and [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] gas [[Mining/Legends|mines]], was [[Occupation of Bespin|occupied]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].<ref name="NEC" /> The [[City/Legends|city]] and mining operations were taken over, but only as small [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] was left.<ref name="SW:BF" /> In addition, although tightly under the control of the Empire, it nonetheless was subject to some minor raids by the Rebel Alliance, with at least [[Raid on Bespin|one]] occurring in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] before the Battle of Endor.<ref name="Rogue Leader">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]''</ref>

==The battle==
===Capturing Cloud City===
[[File:Stormtroopers_CloudCity.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The battle rages in Cloud City.]]
The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] launched an attack on Cloud City. The battle raged both in the air and on the floating settlements of the gas-giant. It started at the capital, and moved outwards across the various gas-refineries as the Rebels continued to gain ground.<ref name="SW:BF" />

Down on the ground, [[Rebel trooper/Legends|Rebel soldiers]] captured the main [[Carbon-freezing chamber/Legends|carbon-freezing chamber]] and secured their command posts in the vicinity. The Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and [[Scout trooper/Legends|snipers]] fell back under the Rebel's attack.<ref name="SW:BF" />

===Capturing the main mining platform===
The [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|SpecForce]] then moved on to capture the main Imperial mining platform.<ref name="SW:BF" />

The Rebel forces landed and secured the gas extractor, and pushed the Imperials back to the other side of the platform. The Rebel forces moved in and captured the Imperial docks and landing platforms, trapping them in the main command center. [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]] bombed the building, [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] destroyed Imperial starfighters before they could lift off, and the enemy numbers dwindled. Teams of SpecForce soldiers moved in on the command building and secured the mining platform.<ref name="SW:BF" />

==Aftermath==
Although the Imperial garrison was attacked,<ref name="SW:BF" /> the city was not [[Liberation of Bespin|liberated]] until after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="ES">''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Elite Squadron]]''</ref> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Expandsect}}
The battle appeared in the [[2004]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]'' in two consecutive Galactic Civil War era missions, although the storyline is said to be [[Canon|S-canon]] by [[Leland Chee]], the Keeper of the [[Lucasfilm]] [[Holocron continuity database]].<ref name="Blog-holocron|56">{{Blog|holocron|56|starwars.com forum HolocronKeeper comments archive}}</ref> The player fights for the Rebellion in both missions. In the second mission, players or the computer may capture neutral Cloud Cars. Although the game's [[Star Wars Battlefront: Prima Official Game Guide|strategy guide]] includes maps of the battle sites, it does not include information on the ''Historical Campaign.''

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]'' {{1st}}
